title: hieracium maculatum subsp. tinctum
intro: Hieracium maculatum subsp. tinctum, also known as spotted hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Hieracium maculatum subsp. tinctum include its potential to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial and fungal infections, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and antiviral agent. Hieracium maculatum subsp. tinctum is typically prepared as a tincture or infusion for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a treatment for various health conditions.
